statement.p: Scientists are conducting an experiment with a gas in a sealed container. The mass of the gas is measured, and the scientists realize that the gas is leaking over time in a linear way. Each minute, they lose 6.6 grams. Eight minutes since the experiment started, the remaining gas had a mass of 217.8 grams.

Let x be the number of minutes that have passed since the experiment started, and let y be the mass of the gas in grams at that moment. Use a linear equation to model the weight of the gas over time.
This lines slope-intercept equation is .
40 minutes after the experiment started, there would be grams of gas left.
If a linear model continues to be accurate, minutes since the experiment started, all gas in the container will be gone.

li.p: A lines equation in slope-intercept form looks like y=mx+b, where m is the slope, and y is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. Since the gas is leaking at a rate of 6.6 grams per minute, we know that the slope of the linear model is -6.6.
So now we have that y=-6.6x+b. The next step is to find the value of b. There are at least two ways.
One way is to substitute a known point into y=-6.6x+b. We use the point (8,217.8).
This lines slope-intercept equation is y=-6.6x+270.6.
Another way is to use the lines point-slope equation y-y_{1}=m(x-x_{1}). Again, we use the point (8,217.8).
This is the second way to find b, and thus the lines slope-intercept equation is y=-6.6x+270.6.
After 40 minutes, we can find the mass of the remaining gas if we substitute 40 in for x in the equation y=-6.6x+270.6. We have:
So after 40 minutes, there are 6.59999999999997 grams of gas remaining.
To find when the gas will be all gone, we need to find when there are 0 grams of gas left. We can substitute 0 in for y in the equation y=-6.6x+270.6, and we have:
So after 41 minutes, the gas will be all gone.
